hero.question: US x Iran ceasefire by April 7?
hero.prob_take: At 11.5%, the market is essentially pricing a US-Iran ceasefire by April 7 as a black swan event, which means you're getting paid pennies on the dollar for tail risk that could materialize fast if either side signals genuine de-escalation. The real question is whether the crowd is anchored to recent tit-for-tat strikes and missing the speed at which these things can flip, or if regional actors are genuinely committed to the current grinding stalemate through early April.

daily_take.headline: Iran Ceasefire Just Cratered. Smart Money Sees It Coming.
daily_take.deck: The US-Iran ceasefire odds collapsed 10.7 points in 24 hours to 11.5%, signaling markets finally priced in what insiders already knew: escalation is the base case. With $13.2M in volume and sellers dominating, this isn't panic dumping, it's conviction. At 11.5%, you're getting asymmetric odds on a black swan outcome. The play depends on your conviction on ground ops (now 21.5% and climbing). If Israel goes in, ceasefire odds evaporate. If they don't, the 11.5% could find bids in a de-escalation surprise.

sidebar.headline: Israel ground op odds jumped 7.5 points. This is your canary. If 21.5% breaks 25%, ceasefire is trading at fair value or rich.
sidebar.label: Israel Ground Op by April 30: 21.5%

sidebar.headline: Rublev getting muppet money at 79.5%. Borges has steadier hands and the odds don't reflect recent form.
sidebar.label: Rublev vs Borges: 79.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz shipping at 15.5% feels optimistic. Regional freight costs suggest traders are pricing in months, not weeks, of disruption.
sidebar.label: Hormuz Normal by End April: 15.5%
